criteria: The agent has read Section 2 completely to understand the MDP formulation for multi-turn code generation, including state, action, oracle reward, and objective (Equation 1).

The agent has read Algorithm 1 to understand the complete iterative training procedure of µCODE, including rollout collection, verifier training, local expert construction, relabeling, and generator fine-tuning.
The agent has read Section 3.2 to understand both verifier loss functions (BCE and Bradley-Terry) and how preference pairs are constructed.
The agent has read Section 3.3 to understand the generator training procedure using the local expert with oracle and learned verifier scores combined via weights β_O and β_L.
The agent has read Section 3.4 and Algorithm 2 to understand the multi-turn Best-of-N inference procedure including the per-turn candidate selection strategy.

The agent has read Section 4.1 completely to check the experiment setup: models (Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct, Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct), datasets (MBPP, HumanEval, CodeContests), baselines, and evaluation metrics.
The agent has read Table 1 to understand the main comparison results of µCODE against baselines across MBPP, HumanEval, and CodeContests with both 1B and 8B models.
The agent has read Table 2 to understand the ablation study on one-step recoverability and verifier types (oracle vs. learned vs. both).
The agent has read Table 3 to understand the comparison of different verifier strategies (Random, LV, PT, PT+LV) for multi-turn BoN search.
The agent has read Appendix C (Tables 6, C.1, C.2) to understand hyperparameters for generator and verifier training, prompt templates, and public-private test split design.
The plan includes the MDP formulation for multi-turn code generation with state s_t = {x, y_1, o_1, ..., y_{t-1}, o_{t-1}}, action y_t, and oracle reward R(x, y_t) = C(x, y_t).
The plan includes the iterative µCODE training loop over M iterations, alternating between rollout collection, verifier training, local expert construction, relabeling, and generator fine-tuning.
The plan includes training the verifier with both BCE loss (Equation 2) and Bradley-Terry loss (Equation 3), and constructing preference pairs (y+, y-) from oracle reward labels for BT.

The plan includes the generator training strategy: constructing the local expert via Equation 4 (linear combination of oracle and learned verifier), building the expert dataset D_* via Equation 5, and fine-tuning the generator on D_*.
The plan includes the multi-turn BoN inference procedure: generating N solutions per turn, selecting the best via verifier (Equation 6), executing the selected solution, and updating the state.
The plan includes loading the MBPP dataset (374 training, 500 test problems), HumanEval (164 problems), and CodeContests (1000 training, 165 test problems) with public-private test splits.
The plan includes implementing the prompt templates for single-step generation and multi-step feedback from Appendix C.1.
The plan includes all three baselines: base Instruct model, single-turn RFT, and multi-turn RFT (fine-tuned on all sub-trajectories of positive rollouts).
The plan includes the training hyperparameters from Table 6: 2 epochs, generator LR 5×10^-7, verifier LR 1×10^-6, generator batch 32, verifier batch 64, generator max seq 4096, verifier max seq 2048.
The plan includes the ablation experiments: comparing µCODE_OV (oracle verifier only), µCODE_LV (learned verifier only), µCODE (both), and RFT_LV (top-K selection by learned verifier).
The plan includes the MBPP POMDP evaluation variant where public unit tests are removed from the prompt to test execution feedback utilization.
The code for multi-turn code environment tracking the interaction history state s_t = {x, y_1, o_1, ..., y_{t-1}, o_{t-1}} has been implemented.
The code for computing the oracle reward R(x, y_t) = C(x, y_t) ∈ {0, 1} by executing code on public and private test cases has been implemented.
The code for rolling out the generator π_θ in the multi-turn environment to collect trajectories D_i = {(x, s_t, y_t, o_t)} has been implemented.
The code for aggregating rollout data across iterations D ← D ∪ D_i has been implemented.
The code for the learned verifier R_φ(x, y) using the base language model with a randomly initialized linear layer to output a scalar score has been implemented.
The code for training the verifier with Binary Cross-Entropy loss as defined in Equation 2 has been implemented.
The code for training the verifier with Bradley-Terry loss as defined in Equation 3, including construction of preference pairs (y+, y-) for each prompt x, has been implemented.
The code for constructing the local search expert π_* as defined in Equation 4 — selecting the code with the highest linear combination of oracle and learned verifier scores — has been implemented.
The code for applying the verifier weight combination with β_O = 1.0 (oracle) and β_L = 0.1 (learned) in the expert construction (Equation 4) has been implemented.
The code for relabeling the collected dataset D with optimal solutions y* to produce the expert dataset D_* = {(x, s_t, y*) | (x, s_t) ~ D} as defined in Equation 5 has been implemented.
The code for fine-tuning the generator π_θ on the expert dataset D_* via supervised fine-tuning has been implemented.
The code for the full µCODE iterative training loop (Algorithm 1) running M iterations and returning the best generator and verifier pair on the validation set has been implemented.

The code for the multi-turn Best-of-N inference (Algorithm 2): generating N rollouts per turn, selecting the best via verifier, executing the chosen solution, updating state, and stopping when public tests pass or turn limit is reached, has been implemented.
The code for verifier-based candidate selection y_t* = argmax_n R_φ(x, y_t^n) during BoN inference (Equation 6) has been implemented.
The code for the PT+LV hybrid selection strategy (preferring solutions that pass public tests, then using learned verifier scores to break ties) has been implemented.
The code for loading and preprocessing the MBPP dataset with 374 training problems and 500 test problems has been implemented.
The code for loading and preprocessing the HumanEval dataset (164 problems, using HumanEvalPack for ease of parsing) has been implemented.
The code for loading and preprocessing the DeepMind CodeContests dataset (1000 training problems, 165 test problems) has been implemented.
The code for the MBPP public-private test split (1 test from the official suite as public, remaining tests and challenge tests as private) has been implemented.
The code for the HumanEval public-private test split (1 test from the code prompt docstring as public, remaining tests plus official test suite as private) has been implemented.
The code for loading and using Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct as the base generator model has been implemented.
The code for loading and using Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct as the base generator model has been implemented.

The code for initializing the verifier from the same base model as the generator (Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct or Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct) with a randomly initialized linear layer for scalar scoring has been implemented.
The single-step prompt template from Appendix C.1.1 (including the {prompt} and {test} placeholders, reasoning instruction, code format instruction, and no-test instruction) has been implemented.
The multi-step feedback prompt template from Appendix C.1.2 providing execution error traces as 'Feedback: {feedback}' has been implemented.
The code for the RFT baseline — collecting multiple rollouts and filtering trajectories with a correct solution for supervised fine-tuning — has been implemented.
The code for the multi-turn RFT baseline — fine-tuning on all sub-trajectories {s_i, y_{i+1}} from positive rollouts — has been implemented.
The code for the RFT_LV ablation — selecting the top K=3 rollouts ranked by the learned verifier for each prompt to use as fine-tuning data — has been implemented.
The generator training uses the hyperparameters from Table 6: learning rate 5×10^-7, batch size 32, 2 epochs, and maximum sequence length 4096.
The verifier training uses the hyperparameters from Table 6: learning rate 1×10^-6, batch size 64, 2 epochs, and maximum sequence length 2048.
The code for running 2 training iterations of µCODE on MBPP and HumanEval, and 1 training iteration on CodeContests, has been implemented.
The code for greedy decoding with temperature=0 for BoN@1 evaluation has been implemented.
The code for sampling with temperature=0.7 for BoN@N evaluation has been implemented.
The code enforcing a maximum of T=3 turns per episode during training and standard evaluation has been implemented.
The code for evaluation with N=5 candidate solutions per turn for the main BoN comparison (Table 1) has been implemented.
The code capping each completion to 1000 tokens per turn during inference has been implemented.
The code for the MBPP POMDP evaluation variant — constructing inputs where public unit test information is removed from the prompt — has been implemented.
The code for the µCODE_OV ablation (β_O=1.0, β_L=0, oracle verifier only for expert construction) has been implemented.
The code for the µCODE_LV ablation (β_O=0, β_L=1.0, learned verifier only for expert construction) has been implemented.
The code for the inference-time scaling experiment varying N ∈ {1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11} candidate solutions per turn has been implemented.
The code for the verifier loss comparison experiment (BCE vs BT) using single-step rollouts from the base model has been implemented.
The code for the Qwen-2.5-1.5B-Instruct model variant training and evaluation on MBPP and HumanEval has been implemented.
The code for evaluating µCODE and baselines on MBPP+ and HumanEval+ (with additional private tests) using the 1B model has been implemented.
The µCODE training (2 iterations) on MBPP for both Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct and Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct has been executed successfully without errors.
The µCODE training (1 iteration) on CodeContests for Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct has been executed successfully without errors.
The evaluation of all methods (Instruct, RFT, µCODE) with BoN@1 and BoN@5 on MBPP, HumanEval, and CodeContests has been executed successfully without errors.
The Table 2 ablation experiments (RFT, RFT_LV, µCODE_OV, µCODE_LV, µCODE) with the 1B model on MBPP and HumanEval have been executed successfully without errors.
The Table 3 experiments comparing Random, LV, PT, and PT+LV selection strategies for BoN search with both 1B and 8B models on MBPP and HumanEval have been executed successfully without errors.
The Figure 2 multi-turn evaluation (up to 6 turns) on HumanEval, MBPP, and MBPP POMDP datasets for all three methods has been executed successfully without errors.
The Figure 3 inference-time scaling experiments with N ∈ {1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11} on MBPP and HumanEval using the 1B µCODE model have been executed successfully without errors.
The Figure 4 verifier loss comparison experiments (BCE vs BT) on MBPP and HumanEval using the base Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct model have been executed successfully without errors.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 1: µCODE with 1B model achieves BoN@5 accuracy of 51.1 on MBPP and 41.5 on HumanEval, outperforming all baselines.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 1: µCODE with 8B model achieves BoN@5 accuracy of 70.6 on MBPP, 63.8 on HumanEval, and 16.3 on CodeContests, outperforming all baselines.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 1: µCODE BoN@1 with 1B model outperforms multi-turn RFT BoN@1 on MBPP (37.9 vs 31.1) and HumanEval (35.4 vs 31.7).

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 1: multi-turn RFT BoN@1 performance drops below the base Instruct multi-turn model on MBPP (31.1 vs 35.1 for 1B), demonstrating the failure mode of sub-trajectory fine-tuning.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 2: µCODE using both verifiers achieves the best performance (51.1 MBPP, 41.5 HumanEval), outperforming all ablation variants by approximately 2%.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 2: µCODE_OV (one-step recoverability with oracle verifier) outperforms RFT baseline on both MBPP (48.2 vs 46.7) and HumanEval (38.0 vs 36.5).
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 3: the PT+LV hierarchical strategy outperforms using PT or LV alone across all methods (Base, RFT, µCODE) and both model sizes.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 3: learned verifier (LV) alone outperforms random selection for all methods and datasets.
The execution outcomes match the results in Figure 2: µCODE BoN accuracy improves over successive turns on both MBPP and HumanEval datasets, demonstrating effective use of multi-turn feedback.

The execution outcomes match the results in Figure 2: in the MBPP POMDP setting, µCODE achieves a 15.9% gain from turn 1 to turn 6 and closes the gap with MBPP, while baselines remain ~6% below their MBPP performance.
The execution outcomes match the results in Figure 3: BoN performance improves with increasing N for µCODE on MBPP (continuous gains up to N=11) and HumanEval (plateaus around N=5).
The execution outcomes match the results in Figure 4: the verifier trained with BT loss consistently outperforms the verifier trained with BCE loss across all values of N on both MBPP and HumanEval.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 4 (Appendix B.1): µCODE with Qwen-2.5-1.5B-Instruct achieves BoN@5 accuracy of 63.1 on MBPP and 74.0 on HumanEval, outperforming Instruct and RFT baselines.
